code: @Manual{,   title = {weightflow: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights},   author = {Juan Pablo Ferreira},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/jpferreira33/weightflow}, }

what: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights
title: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights
text: Declarative, pipeable API build survey weights hierarchical stages, tidymodels-style. starts design base weights chains adjustments: Unknown eligibility — redistributes weight unknown-eligibility cases among known-eligibility ones. Nonresponse — weighting classes (cells) propensity estimated logistic regression (engine = "logit"), CART trees (engine = "tree", via rpart) random forest (engine = "forest", via ranger). Propensities used direct 1/p factor per unit (num_classes = NULL) grouped classes (num_classes = k). integrative calibration — handy members household share weight. Plus model calibration (step_model_calibration, Wu & Sitter 2001): predicts several y variables working models (glm/tree/random forest) calibrates population totals model-assisted efficiency, top calibrating X totals consistency. Plus optional trimming step (step_trim) insertable anywhere cascade, even several times; optional final rounding step (step_round, simple total-preserving); Kish design effect (deff = 1 + CV², effective sample size) reported every stage. plot(fitted) shows diagnostic grid (per-step factor histograms plus summary panel) weight_factors(fitted) returns per-unit, per-step factors custom plots. report_weighting(fitted) writes self-contained HTML report (recipe, requested parameters per step, per-stage summary diagnostics) (per-step plots — weight -vs-scatter adjustment-factor histogram, drawn inline SVG graphics device required) opens browser — Shiny/server needed. Optional bounded calibration (calfun = "logit" bounds = c(L, U) linear method) keeps g-weights within range without separate trim. optional steps: assertions (step_assert, checkpoint errors/warns deff, weight ratio effective n cross threshold), automatic survey-style trimming (step_trim_weights: weight 1, auto upper cap, strict = TRUE like survey::trimWeights), rescaling (step_rescale: normalize weights sample size target total). Response eligibility can supplied 0/1 dummy columns (1 = responded / 1 = unknown) logical condition. Follows classic framework Valliant, Dever & Kreuter (Practical Tools Designing Weighting Survey Samples). computes weights ; estimate variances. inference, export weights use survey/srvyr.

title: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights
text: recipe inert: building computes nothing. prep() walks steps order estimates cascade factors; collect_weights() extracts final weights. Separating define apply makes reproducible auditable.
code: recipe <- weighting_spec(survey, base_weights = pw) |>   step_unknown_eligibility(unknown = unknown_elig, by = "region") |>   step_nonresponse(respondent = responded, method = "weighting_class",                    by = c("region", "sex")) |>   step_trim(max_ratio = 3, reference = "base", redistribute = FALSE) |>   step_calibrate(margins = list(sex    = c(M = 49000, F = 51000),                                 region = c(North = 42000, South = 58000)),                  method = "raking")  fitted <- prep(recipe)          # estimate the cascade summary(fitted)                 # per-stage diagnostics + Kish deff plot(fitted)                    # diagnostic plots wts    <- collect_weights(fitted)   # data.frame with .weight

title: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights
text: installation needed (base R, R >= 4.1):
code: setwd("path/to/weightflow") source("demo.R")          # full household pipeline source("demo_model.R")    # model-assisted calibration, tested against a population

title: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights
text: apply_step() internal S3 generic computes step. add new adjustment: define step_*() constructor (inert) apply_step.<class>() method. Nothing else changes.

title: Declarative API for Staged Survey Weights
text: Valliant, Dever & Kreuter. Practical Tools Designing Weighting Survey Samples. Kish (1965, 1990). Design effect unequal weighting. Potter (1988, 1990); Potter & Zheng (2015); Liu et al. (2004). Weight trimming. Lemaitre & Dufour (1987). Integrative (one weight per household) calibration. Wu & Sitter (2001). Model-calibration / model-assisted estimation.

title: Extract the data with the computed weights — collect_weights
code: collect_weights(   object,   drop_zero = TRUE,   keep_intermediate = FALSE,   weight_name = ".weight" )

title: Extract the data with the computed weights — collect_weights
text: object prepped object (output prep()). drop_zero logical. TRUE, drops rows final weight 0 (ineligible / nonresponse). Default TRUE. keep_intermediate logical. TRUE, adds one column per stage. weight_name name final weight column. Default ".weight".

title: Extract the data with the computed weights — collect_weights
text: data.frame.

what: Kish design effect from unequal weighting — design_effect
title: Kish design effect from unequal weighting — design_effect
text: deff = 1 + CV^2(w) = m * sum(w^2) / (sum(w))^2, active weights. effective sample size n_eff = m / deff.

title: Kish design effect from unequal weighting — design_effect
code: design_effect(w)

title: Kish design effect from unequal weighting — design_effect
text: w vector weights (zeros dropped).

title: Kish design effect from unequal weighting — design_effect
text: list deff, n_eff, cv n.

title: Diagnostic plots for the weights — plot.prepped_weighting_spec
text: x prepped object (output prep()). type "" (default): per-step adjustment-factor histograms PLUS summary panel (final weights, cumulative factor, base vs final, deff stage), one grid. "factors": per-step factor histograms. "summary": summary panel. ... ignored.

text: Writes self-contained HTML file (dependencies, server) showing pipeline, parameters requested step, per-stage summary (n, sum, CV, Kish deff, effective n) per-step diagnostics, opens browser.

code: report_weighting(object, file = NULL, open = TRUE, plots = TRUE)

title: Build a nice HTML report of the weighting recipe — report_weighting
text: object prepped object (output prep()). file output path; NULL, temporary .html file. open logical; open file browser. plots logical; add per-step plots (weight -vs-scatter adjustment-factor histogram). Uses ggplot2 installed, else base graphics.

title: Build a nice HTML report of the weighting recipe — report_weighting

what: Assert conditions on the weights at this point of the cascade — step_assert
title: Assert conditions on the weights at this point of the cascade — step_assert
text: checkpoint change weights; verifies conditions fails (error) warns met. Useful guard production pipeline (tidymodels-style tests inside recipe).

title: Assert conditions on the weights at this point of the cascade — step_assert
code: step_assert(   spec,   max_deff = NULL,   max_weight_ratio = NULL,   min_n_eff = NULL,   on_fail = c("error", "warning") )

title: Assert conditions on the weights at this point of the cascade — step_assert
text: spec weighting_spec. max_deff numeric NULL. Maximum acceptable Kish design effect. max_weight_ratio numeric NULL. Maximum allowed final/base weight ratio (per active unit). min_n_eff numeric NULL. Minimum acceptable effective sample size. on_fail "error" (stop cascade) "warning".

title: Calibration to population totals — step_calibrate
text: Calibration population totals

title: Calibration to population totals — step_calibrate
code: step_calibrate(   spec,   margins = NULL,   method = c("raking", "poststratify", "linear"),   formula = NULL,   totals = NULL,   cluster = NULL,   equal_within_cluster = FALSE,   calfun = c("linear", "logit"),   bounds = NULL,   maxit = 50L,   tol = 1e-06 )

title: Calibration to population totals — step_calibrate
text: spec weighting_spec. margins named list ("raking"/"poststratify"). element named numeric vector target totals per category. E.g.: list(sex = c(M = 5000, F = 5200), region = c(N = 3000, S = 7200)). method "raking" (IPF, categorical margins), "poststratify" (single categorical variable) "linear" (GREG / regression estimator; handles continuous categorical auxiliaries together). formula ("linear") auxiliary formula, e.g. ~ sex + income. Uses model.matrix; includes intercept unless write ~ 0 + ... totals ("linear") named numeric vector population totals, names matching model.matrix columns (including "(Intercept)" = N intercept). names match, error lists expected ones. cluster ("linear") name cluster id column (e.g. "household"), equal weights within cluster. equal_within_cluster ("linear") logical. TRUE, Lemaitre-Dufour (1987) integrative calibration: single weight per cluster. Requires cluster. Final weights equal within cluster provided incoming weight also uniform within cluster. calfun ("linear") distance function: "linear" (g = 1 + u) "logit" (bounded construction). "logit", bounds required. bounds ("linear") numeric c(L, U) L < 1 < U. Bounds calibration factor g (g-weights). "linear" truncates; "logit" enforced smoothly. Avoids extreme/negative weights without separate trimming step. maxit, tol convergence control raking bounded calibration.

title: Model calibration (model-assisted, Wu & Sitter 2001) — step_model_calibration
text: Fits working model study variable y, predicts population, calibrates weights sample total prediction equals population total (model-assisted efficiency). also calibrates X totals (consistency auxiliary controls).

title: Model calibration (model-assisted, Wu & Sitter 2001) — step_model_calibration
code: step_model_calibration(   spec,   x_formula,   models,   population,   cluster = NULL,   equal_within_cluster = FALSE )

title: Model calibration (model-assisted, Wu & Sitter 2001) — step_model_calibration
text: spec weighting_spec. x_formula formula consistency auxiliaries, e.g. ~ sex + region. models named list models created y_model(). names label prediction constraints. population population data.frame auxiliary predictor columns (y variables needed; predicted). cluster name cluster id column (e.g. "household"), equal weights within cluster. equal_within_cluster logical. TRUE, integrative calibration: single weight per cluster. Requires cluster incoming weight uniform within cluster.

title: Model calibration (model-assisted, Wu & Sitter 2001) — step_model_calibration
text: Requires COMPLETE auxiliary information: data.frame population x_formula columns model predictors whole population (reference frame/census).

title: Nonresponse adjustment — step_nonresponse
text: Nonresponse adjustment

title: Nonresponse adjustment — step_nonresponse
code: step_nonresponse(   spec,   respondent,   method = c("weighting_class", "propensity"),   by = NULL,   formula = NULL,   engine = c("logit", "tree", "forest"),   num_classes = 5L )

title: Nonresponse adjustment — step_nonresponse
text: spec weighting_spec. respondent 0/1 dummy column (1 = responded) logical condition (unquoted) TRUE respondents. Eligible cases respondents treated nonresponse. method "weighting_class" (cells) "propensity" (predictive model). character. Adjustment cells method = "weighting_class". formula predictor formula (right-hand side ), e.g. ~ age + region, used method = "propensity". engine engine estimate propensity method = "propensity": "logit" (logistic regression, base R), "tree" (CART via package 'rpart') "forest" (random forest via package 'ranger'). 'rpart' 'ranger' optional: needed pick engine. num_classes integer NULL. Controls propensities used: integer forms many propensity classes (cell adjustment within class); NULL applies direct factor 1/p unit.

title: Rescale (normalize) the weights — step_rescale
text: Rescale (normalize) weights

title: Rescale (normalize) the weights — step_rescale
code: step_rescale(spec, to = c("n", "total"), total = NULL, by = NULL)

title: Rescale (normalize) the weights — step_rescale
text: spec weighting_spec. "n" (weights sum number active units, .e. mean weight 1) "total" (weights sum total). total numeric. Target sum = "total". character. Rescale within groups (optional). = "n", group sums active count.

title: Round the final weights — step_round
text: Optional step, typically last one (calibration). Simple rounding ("nearest") slightly breaks calibrated totals; "preserve_total" uses largest-remainder method keep exact total.

title: Round the final weights — step_round
code: step_round(spec, digits = 0L, method = c("nearest", "preserve_total"))

title: Round the final weights — step_round
text: spec weighting_spec. digits integer. Decimals keep (0 = integers). method "nearest" (simple rounding) "preserve_total" (keeps sum weights). Note: "preserve_total" can break equality weights within cluster; need integer equal weights per household, use "nearest".

title: Trim extreme weights — step_trim
text: Caps weights limit , optionally, redistributes excess among others preserve weighted total (Potter 1988, 1990; Liu et al. 2004). Optional step can inserted anywhere recipe, even several times. Operates CURRENT weights point cascade.

title: Trim extreme weights — step_trim
code: step_trim(   spec,   max_ratio,   min_ratio = NULL,   reference = c("base", "median", "value"),   redistribute = TRUE,   by = NULL,   maxit = 50L )

title: Trim extreme weights — step_trim
text: spec weighting_spec. max_ratio number. Upper cap. meaning depends reference. E.g. reference = "base" max_ratio = 4, weight may exceed 4 times design weight. min_ratio number NULL. Lower floor (units max_ratio). reference "base" (multiple unit's base weight), "median" (multiple median current weights) "value" (absolute weight value). redistribute logical. TRUE, redistributes trimmed excess among uncapped weights preserve total (iterating). calibrate afterwards can use FALSE: calibration restores totals. character. Groups within redistribute (optional). maxit integer. Maximum cap+redistribution iterations.

title: Trim extreme weights — step_trim
text: standard threshold: max_ratio analyst decision, bias-variance trade-. Use Kish's design effect (see summary) judge whether trimming worth .

title: Automatic weight trimming (survey-style) — step_trim_weights
text: Caps weights [lower, upper] redistributes change among untrimmed units preserve total, mirroring survey::trimWeights(). default weight may fall 1, upper cap set automatic empirical rule (Tukey far-fence: Q3 + 3*IQR).

title: Automatic weight trimming (survey-style) — step_trim_weights
code: step_trim_weights(spec, lower = 1, upper = NULL, strict = TRUE, maxit = 50L)

title: Automatic weight trimming (survey-style) — step_trim_weights
text: spec weighting_spec. lower numeric. Lower floor (default 1: weight 1). upper numeric NULL. Upper cap. NULL, automatic rule Q3 + 3*IQR active weights. strict logical. TRUE (default), iterate cap+redistribution weight outside [lower, upper] (like survey's strict = TRUE). FALSE, single pass (redistribution may push weights slightly past cap). maxit integer. Maximum iterations strict = TRUE.

title: Unknown-eligibility adjustment — step_unknown_eligibility
text: Redistributes weight unknown-eligibility cases among known-eligibility cases, within cells defined .

title: Unknown-eligibility adjustment — step_unknown_eligibility
code: step_unknown_eligibility(spec, unknown, by = NULL)

title: Unknown-eligibility adjustment — step_unknown_eligibility
text: spec weighting_spec. unknown 0/1 dummy column (1 = eligibility unknown) logical condition (unquoted) TRUE unknown-eligibility cases. Evaluated data. character. Variables defining adjustment cells (optional).

title: weightflow: declarative survey weighting — weightflow-package
text: Build survey weights design base weights chaining hierarchical adjustments (unknown eligibility, nonresponse, trimming, calibration, rounding, rescaling, assertions) declarative, pipeable, tidymodels-style API. Computes weights ; variance/inference, export weights use 'survey' package.

title: weightflow: declarative survey weighting — weightflow-package
text: Start weighting_spec(), add step_*() adjustments, estimate cascade prep(), extract weights collect_weights(). Inspect summary(), plot() report_weighting().
